threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Overground instead of the Orange Line. The locals know it’s a badge of honour to hate the Tube.
Saw a lad on the 50 last night trying to convince the driver he was an 'Uber for buses.' Got off in front of the Ritzy. Classic Brixton vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When you hear a neighbor serenading the streets with an old accordion, and the sound floats through the open window while you sip your coffee.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that accepts your payment in laughter instead of money—just bring your best joke!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You’ll see locals double-checking if the burrito has cilantro; tourists just dive in, oblivious to the flavor betrayal.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo boarding the 19, cradling a champagne bottle. Only in SF would the Muni be the ride to the gala.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all gentrified cafes, but the real vibe hides in the bare-bones bars where old locals still gather for tapas and cards.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they stand staring at the metro map, trying to make sense of all the lines and colors. They haven’t yet learned that the real map is in their phone.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to Broadway Market instead of just knowing which alley leads there.
Caught a girl on the 59 last night trying to braid her mate's hair with one hand while holding a takeaway in the other. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The sound of a neighbor's saxophone cutting through the morning chaos of a bus strike—classic Neukölln vibes that remind you why you're here.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you hear the U-Bahn drivers announcing stops in a mix of German and Turkish—it's like a mini cultural exchange underground.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they correct you on the pronunciation of ‘Valencia’ while ordering a burrito. Tourists just butcher it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 19 Polk singing opera at the top of his lungs. Tourists were confused, but locals just rolled their eyes and nodded. Classic Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to park Güell but miss the tiny, vibrant plazas in Gràcia where the locals actually kick back with a caña, completely ignoring the real charm.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still stop to take photos of every street corner—like they’re discovering a hidden gem, even in a rainy day.
